title: Reading and Use of English - Part 7
description: You are going to read an article about four people who share their travel experiences and preferences. For questions 43–52, choose from the people (A–D). The people may be chosen more than once.

content.title: Beyond the Map: Four People on Travel and Tourism

texts.D:
### Elena, 29, Photographer
[43]I travel specifically to take photographs – I research destinations for months in advance, looking for the light, the colours, and the stories I want to capture.[/43] Most tourists want warm weather and a beach; I want overcast skies and old market streets. [47]The most memorable trip I've ever taken was to a small village in northern Portugal that almost nobody visits.[/47] There were no tourist menus, no souvenir shops – just local people going about their lives, and I photographed them for three days. That kind of authenticity is impossible to find in popular destinations. [50]I always travel alone because having a companion inevitably means compromising on timing and location.[/50]
texts.A:
### Omar, 45, Sales Director
My job involves a lot of international travel, and I've been to over forty countries. [44]But honestly, most of my trips feel more like office visits in different time zones than real travel.[/44] You arrive, go to a hotel, attend meetings, and leave without ever really seeing the place. [48]I've started deliberately arriving a day early on business trips so I can explore like a proper tourist before the work begins.[/48] It's a small thing, but it has completely changed how I feel about travelling for work. [51]The city I keep returning to is Tokyo – there's nowhere else on earth quite like it, and I discover something new every single visit.[/51]
texts.C:
### Priya, 37, Librarian
[45]I didn't travel abroad until I was thirty, which might seem strange, but growing up my family simply didn't have the money for holidays.[/45] My first international trip was to Morocco and it genuinely changed my perspective on the world. Since then, I try to visit one new country every year. [49]I always stay in locally owned guesthouses rather than big hotel chains because I want my money to go to the community I'm visiting.[/49] I also try to learn at least twenty words of the local language before I arrive – people always respond so warmly when you make the effort. [52]Packing light is a skill I've mastered and I genuinely believe it makes you a better traveller.[/52]
texts.B:
### Ben, 23, Recent Graduate
[46]I spent eight months backpacking across South-East Asia after university and it was the most formative experience of my life.[/46] I went with very few plans and almost no money, sleeping in hostels and eating street food. [53]What I hadn't expected was how often things went wrong – missed buses, flooded guesthouses, getting lost for hours in cities where nobody spoke English.[/53] But looking back, those disasters were the best parts. Each problem forced me to problem-solve and talk to strangers. I came home with skills and confidence I couldn't have gained any other way. I'm already planning my next long trip.

questions.question: plans their trips around the specific goals of their work?
questions.explanation: Elena travels specifically to take photographs and researches destinations months in advance for this purpose.

questions.question: feels that most of their travel does not count as proper tourism?
questions.explanation: Omar says most of his business trips feel more like office visits in different time zones than real travel.

questions.question: had limited travel opportunities when they were younger due to financial reasons?
questions.explanation: Priya says her family didn't have money for holidays, so she didn't travel abroad until she was thirty.

questions.question: describes a long independent journey taken after completing their studies?
questions.explanation: Ben spent eight months backpacking across South-East Asia after finishing university.

questions.question: had their most memorable travel experience in an obscure, little-known place?
questions.explanation: Elena says her most memorable trip was to a small Portuguese village that almost nobody visits.

questions.question: has found a simple strategy to make work trips more enjoyable?
questions.explanation: Omar arrives a day early on business trips so he can explore the destination like a tourist.

questions.question: makes a conscious effort to support the local economy when travelling?
questions.explanation: Priya stays in locally owned guesthouses so her money goes to the community she is visiting.

questions.question: prefers to travel without company in order to have complete freedom?
questions.explanation: Elena always travels alone because having a companion means compromising on timing and location.

questions.question: mentions a particular destination they are repeatedly drawn back to?
questions.explanation: Omar says he keeps returning to Tokyo and discovers something new on every visit.

questions.question: mentions a practical travel skill they are proud of having developed?
questions.explanation: Priya says she has mastered packing light and believes it makes you a better traveller.
